title: Pattern: بدي + verb — "I want to…"
explanation: بدي (baddī) = I want. Suffix changes per person: بدك (you want), بدو (he wants), بدا (she wants), بدنا (we want).

examples.english: I want to go to Beirut
I want to eat something sweet
Do you want to come with me?

title: Pattern: رح + verb — Future Tense
explanation: رح (raḥ) = will / going to. Never changes — same for all pronouns. Replaces سـ or سوف of MSA.

title: Pattern: كنت + verb — "I used to…"
explanation: كنت (kint) = I was / used to. Lebanese past continuous / habitual past.

examples.english: I used to live in Lebanon
We used to go to the sea every summer
I used to love kibbeh so much

title: Pattern: عم + verb — Present Progressive
explanation: عم (ʕam) = right now, currently doing. The Lebanese "-ing."
